assessment_items.question_content:
**What significant life event occurred the year Kahlo painted *The Two Fridas*?** 

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b6f3b16b0660a9336a7ac91fe3831f2f.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Kahlo experienced a number of medical issues throughout her life related to her childhood bout with polio and a serious bus accident at the age of 18.

The year she painted this canvas she was divorced from Diego Rivera, the acclaimed Mexican muralist.

**[Read this text to review.](/humanities/art-history/art-history-1907-1960-age-of-global-conflict/dada/a/kahlo-the-two-fridas-las-dos-fridas)**

choices.content: Her injury in a bus accident

assessment_items.question_content:
**What detail in *The Two Fridas* is frequently seen in Kahlo's work as a symbol of union? **

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b6f3b16b0660a9336a7ac91fe3831f2f.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Exposed anatomy is common in Kahlo's work.  In *The Two Fridas*, the two hearts appear vulnerable and laid bare to the viewer as a testament to her emotional suffering.

Kahlo often used blood as a visceral metaphor of union.  

**[Read this text to review.](/humanities/art-history/art-history-1907-1960-age-of-global-conflict/dada/a/kahlo-the-two-fridas-las-dos-fridas)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**What subject is most common among the 200 paintings Kahlo made in her life time?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
In order to cope with long periods of recovery from her medical condition, Kahlo became a painter. Nevertheless, the isolation caused by her health problems was always present. She reflected, “I paint self-portraits because I am so often alone, because I am the person I know best.”

**[Read this text to review.](/humanities/art-history/art-history-1907-1960-age-of-global-conflict/dada/a/kahlo-the-two-fridas-las-dos-fridas)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which themes are commonly addressed in the work of Frida Kahlo?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
All of these themes are addressed in Kahlo's work.  She flouted both conventions of beauty and social expectations in her self-portraits. These powerful and unflinching self-images explore complex and difficult topics including her culturally mixed heritage, the harsh reality of her medical conditions, and the repression of women.

**[Read this text to review.](/humanities/art-history/art-history-1907-1960-age-of-global-conflict/dada/a/kahlo-the-two-fridas-las-dos-fridas)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**How does Kahlo's *Self-portrait (Time Flies)* differ from her earlier painting *Self-portrait in a Velvet Dress*?**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/626eda42ea8c65288b3de8cad8b9d627.jpg)* Self-portrait (Time Flies)*, 1929

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/35d480e071b3adb9d47cb34c05cdb8e8.jpg) *Self-portrait in a Velvet Dress*, 1926

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Both paintings focus on Kahlo through her central position in a symmetrical composition.

Unlike the 1926 portrait,  *Self-portrait (Time Flies)* highlights Kahlo's Mexican heritage in her accessories that distinct periods in Mexican history.  

**[Read this text to review.](/humanities/art-history/art-history-1907-1960-age-of-global-conflict/dada/a/kahlo-the-two-fridas-las-dos-fridas)**
